base.name: UW-Madison

toefl.note: TOEFL iBT 80+ required. Institution code 1846. 'MyBest' scores and TOEFL iTP are NOT accepted. iBT Special Home Edition is accepted.

ielts.note: IELTS Academic or IELTS Indicator accepted. IELTS General Training is NOT accepted.

duolingo.note: Duolingo English Test 115+ accepted. Search 'Undergraduate' → 'University of Wisconsin–Madison' when submitting.

englishRequirement.note: TOEFL iBT 80+, IELTS Academic 6.5+, or Duolingo 115+ required for international students. Waiver: if you successfully completed a college-level English composition course at an accredited U.S. institution (not a U.S. branch campus abroad), no English test is required. Scores must not be older than two years at time of application.

base.notes: ADMISSION is holistic — no specific course list is required for admission. Minimum 3.0 term GPA, no grade below C, 24+ semester credits (excluding AP/test), ≤80 total transfer credits. Must list a second-choice major outside CoE. Both Fall (deadline Feb 1 priority / Mar 1 final) and Spring (deadline Oct 1) admission available. PROGRESSION (staying in your major): After admission you must meet Progression Requirements — either approved BEFORE transfer (submit supplemental by June 1 for fall) or within first 2 semesters at UW-Madison. The courses listed below are Progression Requirements. Completing them before transfer = much stronger application and guaranteed advancement in your major. Source: engineering.wisc.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer-from-off-campus/

majors.name: Applied Mathematics, Engineering & Physics (AMEP)
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc II (MATH 222), Calc III (MATH 234), Physics I, Physics II. AMEP is administered by the College of Letters & Science — declare internally after admission to UW-Madison. MATH 221/222/234 required with ≥2.75 GPA in those three. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Biological Systems Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc II, Chemistry I, Physics I. Three tracks: Machinery Systems, Natural Resources & Environment, Food & Bioprocess. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Biomedical Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc III (MATH 234), Physics II (PHYS 202/208), Chemistry II (CHEM 104, if CHEM 103 was taken). Competitive program — aim for 3.5+ GPA in technical courses. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Chemical Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc III (MATH 234), Chemistry II (CHEM 104, if CHEM 103 taken), Organic Chemistry I (CHEM 343). Competitive program. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Civil & Environmental Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Engineering Statics (EMA 201). Students may pursue Civil or Environmental Engineering track after admission. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Computer Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc III (MATH 234 or MATH 340 Linear Algebra), CS I (COMP SCI 200 or 300). MATH 375/376 sequence may also fulfill the Calc III requirement. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Electrical Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: CS I (COMP SCI 200 or 300). MATH 375/376 sequence may substitute for Calc III. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Engineering Mechanics
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Engineering Statics (EMA 201). Program focuses on solid mechanics, fluid mechanics, and thermodynamics. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Geological Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Engineering Statics (EMA 201). Combines geology and engineering for subsurface construction, natural hazards, and groundwater applications. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Industrial & Systems Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc III (MATH 234), CS I (COMP SCI 200 or 300). These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Materials Science & Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Chemistry II (CHEM 104, if CHEM 103 taken), Physics II (PHYS 202/208). These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Engineering Statics (EMA 201). One of the most competitive CoE programs — 3.5+ GPA strongly recommended. Minimum grade of C required in Statics and all core ME courses. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.

majors.name: Nuclear Engineering & Engineering Physics
majors.note: PROGRESSION courses: Calc III (MATH 234), Physics II (PHYS 202/208). Covers nuclear science, reactor design, radiation applications, and plasma physics. These are NOT admission prerequisites; completing them before transfer enables progression approval before arrival.
